{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"USER: You are Assessor A. Rank the students best to worst.

GRADE LEVEL CONTEXT: Grade 7
- Vocabulary: Grade-appropriate vocabulary with some variety and accurate word choice.
- Sentence complexity: Mostly simple and compound sentences; some complex sentences.
- Thesis expectations: Clear position or main idea; may be broad but identifiable.
- Evidence expectations: Relevant examples or details; limited depth of analysis.
Rubric (for reference):
# Ontario Writing Rubric (Condensed)

Assess writing on four categories (0-25 points each):

1. Ideas and Content
- Clarity and relevance of main idea
- Supporting details and explanation

2. Organization
- Clear introduction, body, conclusion
- Logical sequence and paragraphing

3. Voice and Style
- Audience awareness and tone
- Word choice and sentence variety

4. Conventions
- Spelling, grammar, punctuation, capitalization
- Sentence completeness and readability

Level guide:
- Level 1 (50-59): limited effectiveness
- Level 2 (60-69): some effectiveness
- Level 3 (70-79): considerable effectiveness
- Level 4 (80-89): high degree of effectiveness
- Level 4+ (90+): exceptional insight and control


Assignment Outline:
Write a formal letter to your principal recommending an environmental action for the school.

Requirements:
- clear thesis/recommendation
- at least three supporting reasons or examples
- formal letter format
- audience-aware tone
- correct conventions for grade-level writing


Student summaries:
- essay1: Dear Principal, I am writing to recommend a school-wide waste reduction plan. Our school community is large, and even small daily habits create a lot of garbage by the end of each week. My first recommendation is to place clearly labeled recycling and compost bins in high-traffic areas. Right now, students often use the closest bin, even when it is the wrong one. Better labels and placement would improve sorting accuracy. My second recommendation is to run a short advisory lesson each month on responsible disposal. Students are more likely to follow routines when they understand why the routines matter. My third recommendation is to track results publicly. If classes can see weekly waste totals, they can set goals and feel proud when they improve. These changes are practical, affordable, a...

Return ONLY a plain text list of the exact student_id strings above, one per line, best to worst.
"}],"type":"message"}
